task_completion.description: Partition the space to separate the kitchen from the dining and bar areas, defining at least four distinct zones (Main Dining, Bar/Lounge, Kitchen, Restrooms).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for walls drawn in the Sweet Home 3D 2D plan dividing the open shell into at least four separate rooms or clearly defined areas.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Main Dining area for at least 40 guests with at least 10 tables and 40 chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Count the tables and chairs in the largest dining zone in the 2D or 3D view. There must be a minimum of 10 tables and 40 chairs visible.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Bar/Lounge with a counter, at least two sofas or lounge chairs, and bar stools.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a distinct bar/lounge area containing a bar counter, bar stools, and at least two sofas or lounge chairs.

task_completion.description: Equip the Kitchen with at least four appliances and three storage units, and install at least two toilets and two sinks in the restrooms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for kitchen appliances (ovens, fridges, etc.) and cabinets/storage in the kitchen area, and check the restroom area for at least two toilets and two sinks.

task_completion.description: Connect functional zones with at least two new doors, and add at least six lighting fixtures and four plants.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least two doors placed in the partition walls, at least six lighting fixtures (ceiling, wall, or floor lamps), and at least four potted plants placed in the layout.

task_completion.description: Create a 3D photo rendering of the completed restaurant and save it to ~/Desktop/bistro_rendering.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Sweet Home 3D 'Create photo' dialog being used, and a terminal or file manager showing the file 'bistro_rendering.png' successfully saved on the Desktop.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Sweet Home 3D application GUI to create the layout.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D interface open with the floor plan being actively edited, rather than just terminal commands or text editors.

integrity.description: Verify the 3D rendering was actually generated from the designed layout.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D rendering dialog or output should visually match the 2D floor plan designed in the GUI, not a downloaded or unrelated image.

integrity.description: Verify the agent built the layout incrementally rather than loading a pre-made completed file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a progression of steps across screenshots (e.g., adding walls, then placing furniture, then rendering) rather than a fully completed layout appearing instantly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses Sweet Home 3D with the starter file 'bistro_restaurant_starter.sh3d'. Note: While the original programmatic verifier had a defect that ignored the 'new_walls' count, the prompt explicitly requires partitioning the space to separate the kitchen from the dining/bar areas. The VLM should verify that distinct zones are created, ideally with visible partition walls. Total points: 100. Pass threshold: 65.
